name: Triple-Chocolate Biscotti
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 tablespoons Finely ground dark-roast coffee beans or instant espresso powder
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F (160°C).
2. Toast the hazelnuts on a baking sheet for 10 to 15 minutes, until they emit a nutty aroma but haven't turned dark brown inside. If skins are still present, cover nuts with a dishtowel or paper towel and rub gently. Let cool.
3. In an electric mixer bowl fitted with a paddle, combine flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, salt, sugar, and coffee. Mix on medium-low speed, then add hazelnuts and chocolate chips.
4. In a separate bowl, lightly whisk eggs and vanilla extract. With the mixer running low, slowly incorporate egg mixture into the dry ingredients until combined. Remove bowl and mix any remaining dry ingredients by hand.
5. Form the dough into four logs (instead of three). Place on baking sheet and bake at 325°F for 30 to 35 minutes, or until sides are firm, tops cracked, and inside no longer looks wet. Reduce temperature to 300°F.
6. Let cool on baking sheet for at least 10 minutes before slicing into 3/4-inch thick pieces. Place slices flat on the sheet and dry in oven for about 25 minutes, pressing a slice lightly to ensure firmness. Transfer to rack to cool.
7. Melt white chocolate over low power or simmering water. Spread evenly on one cut side of each cooled biscotti. Allow to harden before removing from parchment and storing in an airtight container.
